sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Tondela are struggling, having lost three of their last five matches, with only one win in that span. FC Arouca, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last three games, showing resilience with a win and two draws. This suggests Arouca may have the edge in confidence going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Tondela and FC Arouca have each shown competitiveness, with Tondela winning 2-0 in their most recent clash and a 2-2 draw prior to that. Arouca often scores first in these matchups, which could be a key factor today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Tondela, sitting 17th with 9 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 9 goals this season (lowest in the league), while conceding 27. FC Arouca, in 16th with 14 points, have a slightly better attack with 17 goals but have conceded a league-high 39. Defensive frailties on both sides could lead to an open game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Tondela, Maranhão (3 goals) will be crucial in breaking their scoring drought, while Arouca’s Naïs Djouahra and Alfonso Trezza (5 goals each) pose significant threats. Arouca’s attacking output could exploit Tondela’s defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% chance for an away win, combined with Arouca’s recent form and better attacking stats, they appear to be slight favorites. Tondela’s home advantage at Estádio João Cardoso might help, but their poor form makes a turnaround less likely.
